name: Successful Login From a Compromised Host
notes: This rule triggers when a successful authentication is performed on a host that has been categorized as compromized.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
when any of Source Address are contained in any of Compromised Hosts - IP
when at least 2 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 10 minutes
thresholds: when at least 2 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 10 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Successful Login From a Compromised Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Success
AND when any of Source Address are contained in any of Compromised Hosts - IP
AND when at least 2 events are seen with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 10 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Successful Login From a Compromised Host
Event description: A Successful Login from a Compromised Host has been detected.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
